headlineSummary: The day’s Hacker News sentiment is broadly neutral, with a close tie between Google Gemini and OpenAI, Anthropic showing mixed but more substantial discussion, and Microsoft Copilot getting only limited evidence [E7][E3][E4][E1][E9].

ranking.summary: Google Gemini leans slightly negative on limited evidence, driven by a privacy concern about Google storing generated images or hashes and tying them to accounts [E7].

OpenAI is split between criticism about ChatGPT confusing prompts and responses and praise for ChatGPT Pro Deep Research and GPT-5 access, leaving the discussion neutral and closely tied with Google Gemini [E3][E4][E6][E9].
Anthropic has the most discussion and stays mixed-neutral, with complaints about Claude/Claude Code bugs balanced by praise for Claude’s planning, subagents, and value [E1][E2][E4][E5][E8][E9].
Microsoft Copilot gets only limited evidence, with a single positive note folded into a broader value comment about a GitHub plan that likely includes Copilot [E9].

evidence.excerpt: Claude mixes up who said what

annotations.rationale: The title directly says Claude confuses speaker attribution, a model-quality criticism.

evidence.excerpt: Bugginess in the Claude Code CLI is the reason I switched from Claude Max to Codex Pro. I experienced: - rendering glitches - replaying of old messages - mixing up message origin (as seen here) - generally very sluggish performance

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly blames Claude Code/Claude Max for multiple serious bugs and says they switched away because of it.

evidence.excerpt: In chats that run long enough on ChatGPT, you'll see it begin to confuse prompts and responses, and eventually even confuse both for its system prompt.

annotations.rationale: The commenter reports ChatGPT confusing prompts, responses, and system prompt, which is a direct quality criticism.

evidence.excerpt: I prefer ChatGPT Pro Deep Research here As it can quickly survey hundreds of sources for overall relevance - deep dives into specific papers and projects, where an AI coding agent downloads relevant papers and projects for local analysis loops, performs technical breakdowns into essentially a markdown wiki, and then reduces over all of them into a findings report. Claude code is a bit nicer here because it supports parallel subagents well.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says they prefer ChatGPT Pro Deep Research because it can quickly survey hundreds of sources.
The commenter says Claude code is nicer for deep dives because it supports parallel subagents well.

evidence.excerpt: I easily get $1K+ of usage out of my $100 max sub. And that's with Opus 4.6 on high thinking.

annotations.rationale: The commenter strongly praises the value received from the Claude Max subscription and specifically cites Opus 4.6 usage.

evidence.excerpt: Why is it a bunch of mostly unpaid volunteer hackers are putting more effort into supply chain security than OpenAI. I am annoyed.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly criticizes OpenAI for lagging unpaid volunteers on supply-chain security and states annoyance.

evidence.excerpt: Also given that it's Google we are dealing with here, they probably save every single image generated (or at least its neural hash) and tie it to your account in their database.

annotations.rationale: In a Gemini SynthID story, the commenter expresses distrust that Google stores generated images and links them to user accounts, a privacy criticism applicable to Gemini context.

evidence.excerpt: I think anyone who uses Claude knows that it works smarter when you have it make a plan first, and ask it to research the existing code as much as possible first ...

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Claude works smarter with planning and code research, a mild endorsement of capability.

evidence.excerpt: I don't think there's currently better value than Github's $40 plan which gives you access to GPT5 & Claude variants.

annotations.rationale: The commenter praises the value of access to GPT5 through the plan.
The commenter praises the value of access to Claude variants through the plan.
In AI-assistant subscription context, 'Github's $40 plan' most likely refers to GitHub Copilot, and the commenter says there is currently no better value.
